title: geum laciniatum
intro: Geum laciniatum, also known as the cut-leaved avens, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the rose family and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The roots and flowers of the geum laciniatum plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and soothing respiratory problems. Geum laciniatum can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs in traditional folk remedies.
